name: Dequantization
oneliner: Reverse mapping from low-precision quantized values back to approximate floats — happens on-the-fly during inference.
explanation: In W4A16: int4 weights read from memory, multiplied by group scale in registers/shared memory, fed directly into fp16 GEMM. Fused with computation to avoid materializing full fp16 weight matrix — only 4 bits traverse the memory bus. In W8A8: dequantization happens AFTER the integer GEMM (output $\times$ scale product), which is cheaper since it operates on the smaller output tensor. Dequantization is cheap: $O(KN)$ vs $O(MKN)$ for the GEMM itself.
fundamentals: Integer: W_approx = scale $\times$ (W_q - zero_point). Error: |W_original - W_approx| ≤ scale/2. nf4: W_approx = nf4_codebook[index] $\times$ absmax. W8A8 output: Y_fp16 = Y_int32 $\times$ (s_x $\times$ s_w).
